channel.title: EnerTchad — Journal & press releases
channel.description: EnerTchad S.A.’s journal: stories, analysis and milestones in building Chad’s integrated oil chain — from source rock to the pump. Company in formation: dated targets, not producing assets.

item.title: EOR screening: the right method for the right reservoir
item.description: Chemistry, steam or gas? Five reservoir measurements are enough for a first cut: the EOR screening grid, explained simply.

item.title: Early production: converting time into capital
item.description: First barrel in six to twelve months, capital divided by five, on-spec crude from day one: the economics of early production.

item.title: Before the rig arrives: in the boots of a wellpad site manager
item.description: The trade interview: the rig doesn

item.title: The shared rent: what oil must change for the territories
item.description: Local content is not a regulatory constraint: it is our model. Jobs, procurement, training, energy access — the rent that stays in the country.

item.title: Press area: official media kit and Carnets RSS feed
item.description: EnerTchad provides newsrooms with an official media kit — logo, banner, royalty-free photos, FR/EN boilerplate — and opens the RSS feed of its Carnets to follow every publication.

item.title: Anti-fraud notice: no subscription open to date
item.description: The company reminds the public that no payment channel is open: no cash, Mobile Money or “agent” payment can be requested in its name. Official channels are contact@enertchad.td and the official WhatsApp line.

item.title: Investor area: PDF fact sheet and presentation deck
item.description: The investor area now offers a concise PDF fact sheet and the presentation deck: the “source rock to pump” thesis, the targeted 10 M → 1 Bn → 20 Bn FCFA funding cascade, dated milestones and governance.

item.title: The bottle against the axe: LPG versus wood fuel
item.description: Deposit-based LPG, neighbourhood exchange points, clean cooking: every bottle swapped spares trees, gum trees and pastures — biodiversity defended by the business model.

item.title: EnerClub™, EnerPro™ Card, NRJ+™: the station that knows you
item.description: A legible house of brands, from the customer

item.title: Sédigui: the sleeping field that can feed and light the country
item.description: A gas field sleeps instead of creating value: gas-to-power for electricity, a urea/NPK chain for agricultural sovereignty — EnerTchad

item.title: Online catalogue opens: fuels, EnerLub, NRJ+, EnerPro
item.description: The online store presents EnerTchad’s target offer: fuels at the regulated ARSAT price, EnerLub lubricants, NRJ+ LPG and EnerPro services — with quotes via WhatsApp or e-mail. Target offer of a company in formation.

item.title: Drones, smart pigs, crawlers: inspect without stopping, without risking anyone
item.description: In-line inspection, zero human confined-space entry, predictive rather than reactive maintenance: frugal robotics serving asset integrity.

item.title: Train before you drill: the academy that comes before the barrel
item.description: Chad

item.title: The EnerTchad website goes fully bilingual French-English
item.description: The entire corporate site — poles, investors, clients, FAQ, glossary — is now available in English, for international partners along the Doba-Kribi corridor and beyond.

item.title: The distributed reserve: the country that no longer runs dry
item.description: Closed border, flooded road, regional tension: the slightest import disruption can drain entire regions. The answer: a mesh of depots that back each other up.

item.title: The gas-solar hybrid: the field that powers itself
item.description: A field that flares pays twice: it burns its gas and buys its electricity. The solar + associated gas + storage micro-grid, designed for Chad

item.title: Mobile Stations™: the pump that goes to the village
item.description: Where imports don

item.title: The digital field: four layers, from valve to decision-maker
item.description: L1 sensors, L2 data, L3 AI, L4 decision: the sovereign architecture that will run mini-refineries and the network, designed for Chadian terrain.

item.title: The sector Atlas: the data that de-risks the Chadian barrel
item.description: Seven basins, 21 open blocks, dated public sources: the EnerConseils Atlas — data serving those who operate and invest.

item.title: Water-to-Value™: produced water, the second reservoir
item.description: Reinject, close the loop, recover value: how a Sahelian oil company treats the water that comes up with the crude.

item.title: Built to last: integrity, the invisible trade
item.description: A safe well, a clean pipeline, a tight tank: integrity is the trade you only notice when it fails. How EnerTchad intends to make its assets last — from the bottom of the well to the last depot.

item.title: The barrel rolls before the pipe: crude by truck
item.description: Before the pipeline, the truck: how crude trucking unlocks the first barrels of Chad

item.title: Natron, neem, cotton: EOR chemistry sourced in Chad
item.description: Recovering more oil with the country

item.title: The modular mini-refinery: refining close to demand
item.description: 500 to 2,000 b/d skid-mounted trains, relocatable and data-driven: a plant that follows demand, not the other way round.

item.title: Doba–Kribi: the economics of a 1,070 km pipe
item.description: Landlocked oil, a cross-border pipeline, a transit tariff: how export logistics shape the value of every Chadian barrel.

item.title: The additional barrel: the oil we thought was lost
item.description: ASP, gas, steam: how enhanced oil recovery wakes up known fields — and why it makes particular sense in Chad, with locally sourced inputs.

item.title: The "28-32°" riddle, finally solved
item.description: Chad does not have one crude — it has several. Why acidity, not density, is the parameter that decides how you refine Doba.

item.title: Fluid mechanics, from reservoir to pump
item.description: Making a reluctant crude flow: Darcy, nodal analysis, Reynolds and rheology, explained on the Chadian chain.

item.title: What is the price of a litre at the pump made of?
item.description: Crude, refining, logistics, taxes: the four layers of the pump price, explained simply — and what integration changes for Chad.

item.title: EnerTchad S.A. corporate website goes live
item.description: EnerTchad S.A., an integrated Chadian-owned oil company in formation, launches its corporate website: the “source rock to pump” chain, seven poles, the 2030 targets and the “Train before extracting” approach.
